sections.paragraph: (“high places,” see נָבָה ), [Nebaioth], pr.n. Nabathæa the Nabathæans, a people of northern Arabia, of the race of Ishmael ( Gen 25:13 28:9 ), possessed of abundant flocks ( Isa 60:7); also living by merchandize and rapine (Diod. ii. 48; iii. 42). (Arab. نَبَطُ and نَبِيطُ in which ט has sprung from the ת servile of the Hebrew.) Relandi Palæstina, p. 90, seq.
